entry.title: SAP Inside Track Wroclaw 2024
entry.content:
Join the next SAP Inside Track in Wroclaw in 2024! An SAP Inside Track is a community event, open for all people from the Community (Customers, Consultants and Freelancers). No matter if you are a developer, business consultant or an architect. The event is organized by peers from the community.. and YOU are able to influence the agenda.

Read this post from @anwilk and register now! 

*Please note that this event entry is a summarized version of the original event and your registration may be required by the organizer shared on the original event website. RSVP accuracy here is without guarantee and does not constitute registration. However, if you want to earn an SAP Community badge, please leave your RSVP here as well.

entry.title: SAP ECC PM : IW32 released order and change WBS element
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm working on plant maintenance work orders in IW32, there is WBS element in additional data tab but these are empty or false : the orders are released and WBS element is greyed out.

is there a way to change WBS element ( as these are greyed ) ?

thanks a lot.

Best regards,

Romeo.

entry.title: Re: Our Way into the Clean Core
entry.content: Thanks for a really interesting read from a number of perspectives, to see how an SAP partner approaches this challenge, and learning how you are planning to gradually phase to a clean core, this can also become a roadmap for SAP customers too.

entry.title: Re: CDS View for Check Clearing Date
entry.content:
Hello, 

As answered before for the same question. 

To retrieve the "Check Clearing Date" field in a Custom CDS View in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, you can look into the standard CDS View FINS_ACDOCA.

The "ABLAD" (Clearing Date) field in this CDS View represents the "Check Clearing Date" that you are looking for. You can include this field in your custom payment report by enhancing the standard CDS View or creating a new custom CDS View based on FINS_ACDOCA.

I hope this helps. Good luck with your report customization!

 

BR
Harish Mangtani

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: Hi from SAP in Melbourne Australia. I started working with SAP in the late 90s on a Y2K project and have never looked back. I enjoy using innovation to improve business processes and have had the opportunity to work around the world. At the moment I’m focused on SAP Business Network and look forward to being part of the community

entry.title: Transition to SAP Cloud ALM – We’ve Got the Solution
entry.content:
After being a mainstay of SAP’s strategy for multiple decades, mainstream maintenance for SAP Solution Manager is due to expire at the end of 2027. While this may seem like a “tomorrow problem” at first glance, it’s safe to say this isn’t a long time in the world of business software. For users of this well-established product, SAP therefore recommends transitioning to SAP Cloud ALM well before this date.

The good news is that customers are not alone, SAP is on hand to provide the support and tools needed to prepare and execute this transition. At the upcoming SAP ALM Summit EMEA 2024 in Mannheim, Germany (Sep 24-27), there will be a dedicated track of sessions outlining exactly what you need to consider, and how to go about it. To give you a small taste, expect to hear all about Solution Documentation, Test Management, Change Request Management, and first-hand experiences from SAP customers. Read on to find out more!

Functional overview of our SAP solutions

To guide customers through the transition and make it as smooth as possible, SAP is breaking it down into distinct stages for our customers, primarily zooming in on the first two columns shown below:

As you’ll see, already today customers can – and should – consider adopting SAP Cloud ALM for operations and SAP Cloud ALM for service. Not only will this minimize their update efforts, crucially it will simplify their monitoring tasks and ease their use and consumption. Going forward, customers can then adopt SAP Cloud ALM for implementation at their own pace as functionality is added progressively throughout this year and beyond.

Beyond that, customers may also consider adding SAP Focused Run if they need to monitor large landscapes or high volumes of data.

 

Kicking off the transition with the SAP Readiness Check

If you look at the overview graphic below, you’ll see the journey starts with the SAP Readiness Check. The latest version of the SAP Readiness Check was just released on June 12, 2024, so now is the ideal starting point to work out what your current usage is, and how the transition can benefit you even further.

High-level overview of the transition to SAP Cloud ALM

SAP will be providing various recommendations depending on what customers are using their SAP Solution Manager for. The last thing anyone wants is any nasty surprises, so customers must ensure they read all the available information carefully to assess their own situations. If you’re still not sure where to start, the Transition section on the SAP Support Portal is a great place to begin. Also keep your eyes open for an SAP Cloud ALM transition roadmap based on SAP Activate Methodology to guide our customers through the transition.

 

Migrating Data with the Selective Data Transfer

The plan is for our customers to use the Selective Data Transfer (SDT) to migrate their relevant data from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM. Once the right tools are available, they should then be able to define and tailor their scope flexibly, ensuring that their important data is retained in their future setup. Customers will be able to use the SDT for certain data as soon as it is available and then going forward, they stand to benefit from the full potential offered by the SDT. At the same time, it’s also worth remembering that monitoring and operations capabilities require no data migration at all.

Once available, the plan is that SAP Solution Manager customers on SP19 will be able to call up the SDT and even the Transition section of the SAP Support Portal directly from within their launchpad (customers on certain earlier SPs should also be able to access the SDT via a transaction instead of the launchpad).

Handling Change Control Management

Regarding the Change Request Management (aka. ChaRM) needs of our customers, note that data migration is not provided. SAP’s advice here is to follow the SAP Cloud ALM roadmap until everything you need is there, while already familiarizing yourselves with Deployment Management in SAP Cloud ALM. And for any customers not currently using ChaRM at all, this may be the ideal opportunity to improve their software logistics and stabilize their environment by using the Deployment Management features offered by SAP Cloud ALM. 

 

Closing Words

As your main take-away, just keep in mind that this transition needs to be tackled sooner rather than later, and that SAP is on hand to support customers every step of the way with the following tools and guidance:

Find out more at the SAP ALM Summit EMEA 2024 this September in Mannheim, so sign up today to join us and other top experts. Feel free to share this within your community network and get involved. Thanks as always for your time!

entry.title: Oracle tables not available in BW/4HANA(under HANA_SDA option) post connection.
entry.content:
Hi Experts, we have connected Oracle system to BW/4HANA system via SDA. However, the table under the desired schema isn't visible. In RSA1, all the system connection looks fine. What could be the possible reason? Your help is greatly appreciated.

Thank You.

#SDA #BW/4HANA #Oracledatabase

entry.title: Remove the ability to delete a purchasing order in Fiori App Manage Material Coverage
entry.content:
in S/4 public cloud - In the tile “Monitor Material Coverage – individual/net segment” if I click on “Stock availability” then “Check Material Coverage” I switch to another tile called “Manage Material Coverage”.

Here, it’s possible to delete a purchasing order. We would like to remove this possibility.

in S/4 PUBLIC Cloud the catalog which allow to access to the tule “Monitor material coverage – Net/Individual” and “Manage Material Coverage” is :

  • SAP_SCM_BC_MRPCOCKPIT_MC

Do you think it's possible to remove the ability to delete a purchasing order with restriction while retaining access to the tile and all other features?

SAP Fiori Cloud

entry.title: Destination for Live Process Projects
entry.content:
Hi,

I struggle to get the Destination for SAP Build Process Automation Live Projects Working, described here https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/configure-destination-for-live-process-projects?locale=en-US.

The destination looks like this:

When I check the connection it returns "Connection to "WM_CF_SPACE_PROVIDER" established. Response returned: "401: Unauthorized" ", so I assume that the URL is correct and its a matter of the user.

The User, used for the destination,  is available in the Subaccount and as a space member with role "Space Developer"

Could you please help me what is missing? Many thanks!

Henrike

entry.title: Re: The ABAP Detective Investigates Noise on the Wire
entry.content:
(continuing the investigation into noise on the wire)

With symptoms noted previously, I speculated on possible root causes, and how to decide which detective steps to take, in which order. Of the basic network challenges, one phrase stuck in my mind: "It's DNS. It's always DNS." Using that as a possible smoking gun, I switched the server/agents connections from FQDN (fully qualified domain name) to IP address (which Zabbix allows nicely).

No change to the issue.

Similarly, agent connectivity for this suite has "active" and not active configurations. Also easy to change, so that was fix try #2. No effect.

Third search direction: assume there is a traffic contention issue and turn off everything but the minimal sandbox I set up that duplicated the issue. Two ways I could do this: gradual, by disabling agents one-by-one, or in larger batches, or just shut off the "production" servers running on the local network. That was brute force, which in a shared environment would require planning and permissions, but for a single user landscape, a risk worth taking (minimal business impact).

A few minutes later, I logged the last network anomaly message:

  4857:20240616:231310.765 resuming Zabbix agent checks on host "nbhp": connection restored

An early lesson I learned in the "deploy an agent and poll for data" matrix was "be patient" as the re-sample cycle wheels turned and the root-cause-analysis algorithms build up their caches. After several cycles when agent connections had dropped before, no messages meant success (or at the least, progress).

Like earlier, I went from logs to graphics and viewed the results. First view, looking good!

Noise fix image 1

Two agents, now both at 100% availability. Here patience is required. Let's wait a few more cycles and check that no backwash happened.

Noise fix image 2

Yes, continued lack of noise and possible data loss. Now what? To verify this change was as effective as it seemed, add back in the shut-down systems. Ah, another connection failure after the restart.

Noise fix image 3

Definite progress finding the likely root cause, though not the full details (yet). The next morning, with only one noise-inducer running (out of 3 running prior), the change to the system behavior is evident in the charts.

Noise fix image 4

Each red or green spike to the left of the arrow (when systems shut off) is a failure, to communicate. To the right of the arrow are many fewer drops since.

Case closed? Not quite yet, as we have a suspect. Just need a confession, and a motive.

<to be continued!>

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano

Currently I landed job as ERP Project Manager and looking to receive second exam attempt for C_ACT_2403 - SAP Certified Associate – Project Manager - SAP Activate. 

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Kind Regards,

Mohamed Khorshed

entry.title: How to Migrate on-premise SAP marketing cloud data to any other cloud system?

We are using SAP Hybris Marketing cloud on-prem and we want to migrate all the data & permission present in the system to some other cloud product.

Can anyone suggest what is the easy way to do it?

We were thinking of writing a program in backend (ABAP) and then extract the data in files. But there is millions of data present so this should be the last option if we are not able to find any easy solution.

Any lead would be helpful.

Thank you!

entry.title: Need BW, BO, HANA , Memory and Run time statistics
entry.content:
Please let me know how to get Time and date, User name, Process type(process chain, BW Report, BO Report, HANA Views ), Memory consumed, Duration it took using HANA SQL and any other method.

We need to immediately understand which PC OR Bex Report, or HANA View is causing performance issues.


Regards,
Naiduz

entry.title: Streams in RAP (managed BO), how to keep stream content in connected DMS rather than in database
entry.content:
Hi all,

we have implemented a SAP RAP based app (managed scenario) that allows for uploading of attachments to some datamodel defined in a CDS Data Model to which the attachment entity is a composition child. As per RAP Solution Design the file content (Stream) get's stored in the SAP Database in a field of the the data base table stated in 'draft table' (while in draft state) and 'persistent table' (after draft becomes active entity). 

Our requirement is to store the stream content at time of draft action active and store it in the Document Management System connected to the backend instead of in the persistent table. The problem: when the stream ist meant to be downloaded from the Fiori Elements app the 'Select' statement executed in CL_SADL_SQL_EXECUTOR-> SELECT assumes the data to in the persistent table and there is no way I could find to supplement the stream data from the connected Document Management System instead.

Did you come accross this requirement in the context of managed RAP BOs using streams? How did you solve? I'd appreciate your insights.

Thanks,

Stefan

entry.title: Re: S4CLOUD : Adobe Form - Purchase Order Item Text not D...
entry.content:
Hello

 It seems like there might be an issue with the binding or the form configuration. Have you checked the binding properties to ensure that it is correctly linked to the PO item text? It's also important to verify if the PO item text data is being correctly fetched from the backend system.

If the binding and data fetching are all set up correctly, then you may need to review the form configuration to ensure that the PO item text is included in the output. Sometimes, certain fields may be hidden or incorrectly configured in the form layout.

some steps you can follow to debug and analyze the issue with the custom form for the purchase order in S/4 HANA Public Cloud 2402:

1. Check the binding: Start by checking the binding of the PurchaseOrderHeaderTextElement to the PO item text. Ensure that the binding is correctly configured and that it is referencing the correct field in the data source.

2. Check data source: Verify that the data source for the PO item text is correctly maintained and that the relevant data is available for the form output. You can do this by checking the data source in the backend system or using debugging tools provided by S/4 HANA.

3. Test in a different context: Try using the PurchaseOrderHeaderTextElement in a different context or form to see if the issue is specific to the custom form or if it is a broader data-related problem.

4. Debugging tools: Utilize the debugging tools provided by S/4 HANA, such as the Built-in debugger or the SAP Fiori Elements app to inspect the data and the form output. This can help you identify any issues in the data or the form configuration.

Let me know if this help.

 

BR

Harish Mangtani

entry.title: And Next on Stage is...
entry.content:
The SAP Sapphire event in Orlando has just wrapped up, marking the end of a fantastic week filled with innovation, insightful sessions, and meaningful networking opportunities. Attendees experienced a showcase of SAP’s latest advancements and strategies, making it a memorable and impactful event. As the excitement from Sapphire continues to resonate, anticipation builds for the next installment of Discover SAP YouTube series, set to premiere on June 19th with Christian Freytag, Chief Data and Technology Officer at SAP IT. Learn how our digital IT organization supports our business as we move to cloud-based business models and evolve our enterprise architecture strategy.

The first session with the Senior Director of SAP Global Experience Technology at SAP IT, Oliver Gutzeit launched just a few weeks ago and in case you missed it, you can catch the replay on the SAP YouTube Channel. Looking ahead, the Discover SAP series has two more exciting sessions lined up for July 2024 hosted by one of the Cloud Solution Architects in the Platform Center of Excellence, Alexandra Hofmann. These will give some insights into how we enable all SAP developers with foundational governance within SAP Business Technology Platform and how we implement SAP Build solutions to accelerate business. These and the following sessions in fall of 2024 will delve further into practical applications, provide some hands-on demos on how SAP uses its own solutions and give a glimpse into innovative solutions, ensuring that the momentum from Sapphire continues throughout the whole year Stay tuned for these insightful episodes that will keep the SAP community engaged and informed.

 The “Discover SAP” series is a joint event by SAP’s IT and SAP Global User Groups Organization. Crafted and presented by SAP IT experts themselves, the series gives the inside scoop, guaranteeing real, in-depth insights into the inner workings of the digital landscape. We shine a light on SAP’s uniqueness, highlighting the capabilities and possibilities of our technology and solutions. We leverage the power of storytelling and show how SAP runs. 

Visit the Discover SAP webpage, register for the series, join the sessions, and come along for the ride. Discover SAP isn’t just a show; it's an experience. So, let’s dive into the world of SAP together!

entry.title: DataSphere - Replication Flow : Enabling the ABAP CDS Extractor
entry.content:
SAP Datasphere Replication Flow:

SAP Datasphere has introduced a new feature, 'Replication Flows’ which allows for the process of copying multiple tables/extractors from one source to another, offering a fast and seamless experience in data management. For detailed insights into replication flows and their functionalities, please refer below link

Configure Your Replication Flow | SAP Help Portal

Scenario –

In the current landscape, SAP Data Services is used for moving the SAP ECC Data to Azure Data Lake. The customer was moving to S4HANA and wanted to use SAP DataSphere for analytics and loading data to Azure Data Lake.

Datasphere is the best tool for analytics and data warehousing. After technical discussion , SAP Datasphere replication flow was finalized to move the data from S4HANA to Azure Data Lake. We did do the POC using the Datasphere trial version, and all looked fine.

Creating Connection in Datasphere:

Connection in Datasphere

Issue: In Datasphere replication flow, extractor enabled ABAP CDS view were not available

Solution:

The extractor enabled ABAP CDS views were not visible when replication flow was selected for development. These views were available if we select the option for Import Entities in Data Builder. I checked lot of forums and raised a query.

SAP Community - Query

The issue was also raised in SAP Customer Influence:

SAP Customer Influence

The below SAP note had to be implemented to resolve the issue. We raised the issue with SAP as well and they also asked us to apply the SAP Note. Please go through the note as it talks about implementing 60+ SAP Notes.

3130827 - SAP Data Intelligence - ABAP Integration - SAP S/4HANA 2022

After the note implementation the issue was resolved.

I will come up with a step-by-step blog on loading data from S4HANA to Azure Data Lake using Datasphere replication flow. Currently working on it 😊

Helpful link:

3297105 - Important considerations for SAP Datasphere Replication Flows

3471218 - Datasphere - CDS View list in Data Flow and Replication Flow

entry.title: Re: Creating Custom BO with upload function like 'Import...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Yes it is possible, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition,

Option 1 via In-app extensibility:

It is possible to create custom business objects with upload functions using Key User Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Here are the steps to guide you:

1. Go to the Extensibility Pane in the SAP Fiori launchpad and select the Custom Fields and Logic app.

2. Click on the "New" button to create a new custom business object.

3. Enter the details for the new business object, such as the name, description, and relevant fields.

4. Once the business object is created, you can add fields to it and define the upload function by adding an attachment field.

5. Use the "Add Field" button to add the attachment field to the business object.

6. Define the properties and settings for the attachment field, such as allowing multiple file uploads and setting the file size limits.

7. Save your changes and activate the custom business object.

8. Test the upload function by creating a new record for the custom business object and attaching files to it.

This way you can create a custom business object with an upload function similar to the Import Supplier Invoices App in S/4HANA Cloud. This will allow you to extend the functionality of your system and meet your specific business needs.

Option 2 via Developer Extensibility:

follow these steps to create a custom business object with an upload function using Developer Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud:

Step 1: Define the custom business object
- Navigate to the Extensibility app in SAP Fiori launchpad.
- Select "Custom Fields and Logic" and then click on "Custom Business Objects".
- Click on "Create" and define the custom business object by specifying its name, description, and other relevant details.

Step 2: Add attributes to the custom business object
- Once the custom business object is created, click on "Add" under the "Fields and Logic" tab.
- Add the required attributes for the custom business object. For example, if you want to create an upload function for supplier invoices, you can add attributes such as Invoice Number, Invoice Date, Supplier Name, Amount, etc.

Step 3: Define the upload function
- Under the "Actions" tab, click on "Add" to define the upload function.
- Specify the name and details of the upload function. For example, you can name it "Upload Invoices" and provide a description.

Step 4: Implement the upload logic
- Once the upload function is defined, you can implement the logic for uploading supplier invoices. This can be done using SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) or directly in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system using relevant APIs and services.

Step 5: Test and deploy the custom business object
- After implementing the upload logic, thoroughly test the custom business object to ensure it functions as expected.
- Once validated, deploy the custom business object to make it available for use in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system.

Keep in mind that the exact implementation may vary based on specific requirements and system configurations. It is recommended to refer to the official SAP documentation and consult with SAP experts for a more detailed and tailored approach.

BR

Harish Mangtani

entry.title: Re: Unable to deploy xsuaa service with xs-security.json
entry.content: { "xsappname": "ccstemplate", "tenant-mode": "dedicated", "scopes": [ { "name": "$XSAPPNAME.Admin", "description": "Admin" } ], "attributes": [], "role-templates": [ { "name": "Admin", "description": "generated", "scope-references": [ "$XSAPPNAME.Admin" ], "attribute-references": [] } ], "oauth2-configuration": { "credential-types": [ "binding-secret", "x509" ], "redirect-uris": [ "http://localhost:5000/**", "https://*.*.hana.ondemand.com/**", "https://*.launchpad.cfapps.*.hana.ondemand.com/**" ] } }

entry.title: Cross-Origin Theming for Classic UIs
entry.content:
Since December '23, the UI theme designer on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment, allows to Add Cross Origin Preview Pages to the UI theme designer, as theming previews. Those applications have to implement a special interface, described in the tutorial Add A Cross-Origin Preview for the UI Theme Designer, and will then display theme changes in real time.

Today I'm happy to announce that, starting with Cumulative Patch for UR-c2 2406, applications built with Classic UIs can be used as cross-origin theming previews, i.e. applications based on SAP GUI for HTML, or based on Web Dynpro ABAP. UR-c2 implements the described interface and, with that, displays theme changes immediately.

This theme change preview is even faster than using regular UR c2 preview applications that are part of UI theme designer. That is because they only depend on a very small subset of theming parameters (the theming-base-content). So the majority of theming happens at runtime in the browser, whereas for regular UR c2 previews the whole CSS has to be compiled for every theme change.

However, due to the nature of cross-origin embedded applications, the control picker of the Expert panel does not work. That is because the control picker has to analyze the picked element, and, since the application is embedded cross-origin, the browser does not allow this analysis for security reasons.

So: follow SAP Note 2500800 Cumulative Patches for Unified Rendering (UR-c2 and Lightspeed) to install the latest UR-c2 for your Classic UI backend, and start using your Classic UI applications as theming previews today! Then let me know in the comments how that went.

entry.title: Re: Guide in a needed middleware integration with SAP

entry.title: Automatic account assignement on Purchase Order Items ( SAp ByD )
entry.content:
Hello ByD Experts,

Is it possible to automatically enter the right GL account for purchase order items.
For exemple, if company A buys an item from Company B, the GL account assignement will be done automatically depending if the item is non-stock or if it is a material or a service.

Thank you in advance.

Best Regards.

entry.title: Re: SAP Trial installation stalling on unbuntu
entry.content: Ubuntu works also, no problem

entry.title: Re: Is PO Item Text output within form can be controlled...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Yes it is possible in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Sytem.

1. Log in to your S4 HANA public cloud system and navigate to the Purchase Order application.

2. Go to the "Form Template Maintenance" app.

3. Select the relevant form template for the purchase order.

4. In the form template, locate the section where the purchase order item text is displayed.

5. Click on the "More" button or right-click on the section to access the settings for that specific field.

6. Look for an option to set restrictions based on the purchase organization.

7. If there is an option available, select the appropriate purchase organization and set the restriction to not allow printing for that organization.

8. Save the changes to the form template.

9. Test the restriction by creating a purchase order for the specified purchase organization and attempting to print the purchase order item text.

10. If the restriction is working as expected, the printing of the purchase order item text will be restricted for the specified purchase organization.

BR

Harish Mangtani

entry.title: Re: Material master upload
entry.content:
Hi Anand,

Hope this message finds you hale and healthy!

I have a similar requirement to use LSMW for uploading attachments to material master. Could you please tell me which BAPI you used in your case.

Thank you,

Poongodi Elangovan.

entry.title: Unable to suppress group footer section based on condition
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have created one SAP crystal report. In this report I have created one group based on row headers. There are 4 row headers so 4 sections are created.(suppose the sections are A,B,C,D)

I have suppressed details section.

In this main report I have created 4 sub reports. I have enter this reports in group footers. I want to print this sub report for each row header. example. 1st sub report should print for A row header , 2nd sub report should print for B row header.

I am trying to achieve this but the issue is all the 4 reports are printing for all 4 row headers.

Could you please help on this issue ?

 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing - Customization for Messages without Integration Workflow Configuration
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

For the message “Inspection Results Recording” there is no Workflow Configuration and we have following problem:

These are the CI Artifacts triggered from this Message.

We need to update the Bapi Service Processor (BAPI_INSPOPER_RECORDRESULTS) to send the SFC as parameter EXT_NO (Single-Unit Number for the Unit to Be Inspected).

What is the best way to make this kind of customization?

Another question: Is it possible to trigger another IFlow for this message? (without using an Automatic Trigger/Production Process Designer)

Thanks in advance

Gabriela

entry.title: Re: Failed to initialize application data service - Error 0 (Unknown); GET https://webclient
entry.content: Hi Jitendra , Any other possible recommendations with this issue. I tried all the steps you mentioned in the replies. For me it does not even work for a new application that I kick started with a template project. I choose MDK project and a custom destination from the Mobile services. Any insight would be helpful. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori UI Adaptation: Hide 'Standard' View
entry.content:
Hello, 

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can achieve this requirement by using the Manage Variants functionality. Here's  how to prevent the user from selecting the Standard variant and display only the Custom variant for the Roles table:

1. Navigate to the app where the smart table object is located.

2. Click on the 'Variant Management' button on the toolbar of the smart table. This will open the Manage Variants dialog.

3. In the Manage Variants dialog, select the Custom variant that you have created for the Roles table.

4. Click on the 'Set as Default' button to set the Custom variant as the default variant for the smart table.

5. Next, click on the 'Authorization' tab in the Manage Variants dialog. Here, you can restrict the access to the Standard variant for specific roles or users. You can remove the authorization for the Standard variant for all users or specific roles.

6. Once you have set the Custom variant as the default and removed the authorization for the Standard variant, click on 'Save' to apply the changes.

this way you can prevent the user from selecting the Standard variant and display only the Custom variant for the Roles table in the Fiori app. This will ensure that the fields hidden in the Custom variant will not be accessible to the user.

BR

Harish Mangtani

entry.title: Re: SAP SCREEN PERSONAS - SAP EWM WAREHOUSE MONITOR
entry.content:
Hello

Yes, it is possible to disable a button within the Warehouse Monitor app in S/4 HANA Public Cloud based on movement type and applicable for certain users.

To do this, you would need to use the SAP Fiori UI adaptation tool to customize the Warehouse Monitor app. You can use the UI adaptation tool to configure the visibility and behavior of UI elements, such as buttons, based on certain conditions.

First, you would need to identify the specific movement types and users for which you want to disable the button. Then, using the UI adaptation tool, you can create a rule that checks the movement type and user information, and based on that rule, you can disable the button for the specified conditions.

The UI adaptation tool provides a user-friendly interface for making these customizations without the need for extensive coding. Once the rule is created and applied, the button will be disabled as per your specified conditions.

It's important to note that making these customizations may require appropriate authorizations and access to the UI adaptation tool. 

BR
Harish Mangtani

entry.title: Re: Not authorized to create custom business object ZZ1_Y...

Hello, 

Can you contact me directly on SAP email ? - h.mangtani@sap.com

As this is an internal system issue, so i can help.


BR

Harish Mangtani

Can I disable this check with some property?

entry.title: LICKING THE GIF CYCLE
entry.content:
There are failures, and then there are failures. In hardware, the smoke test often gets you one chance. In software, you get do-overs. In art creation, you can go with the first take, or refine, or toss and start over. This failure story, is of the last kind. When I dog-fathered the #SCNotties contest here in a prior decade, the rule was more or less, be funny and be quick. Fast, as in a 30-second elevator pitch. Today, a 3 second You-Tube yell-at-you.

Roughly 15 years (or 5 SAP web app cycles) ago, I put together an animated GIF selfie using tools I had available (motto: "when you have a hammer, every task needs to be nailed"), in this case, X Windows, NetBSD, XPaint, and the aptly named gifsicle.

Here you might be saying, but Jim, (or Detective, depending on which side of the desk you're on), that is not coding. How can you toss a tiny image into a global, enterprise-scale contest, and not have impostor feebies?

Link to the post that has the image, preserved within the SAP community domain--https://community.sap.com/t5/additional-blogs-by-members/30-seconds-over-the-hill/ba-p/12874805

And what's so funny about a GIF?

What I said then was, " I created it on my NetBSD workstation using XPaint and gifsicle, and the Jimbert character Blag and I created for his comic strip, in about 90 minutes of hacking." Let's consider hacking as a synonym for coding. Many people I've met do what one may call configurating not coding. It's similar mental effort.

 

gifsicle pic1.gif pic2.gif pic3.gif > animation.gif

 --delay time

bash-4.0$ gifsicle --delay 100 cpu_micro_pool_stacked*gif >animation.gif
gifsicle: warning: so many colors that local colormaps were required
gifsicle: (You may want to try `--colors 256'.)

 

The bash version should give you a time-capsule sense of when I tried this, and the warnings hint at the limited palette for graphics icons. Delay is key to the animation working, the 100 here has no bearing on how I drew myself. Possibly on an archive somewhere I have the individual GIF files I rolled together to make the final public version, though if you want to know, the composite can be disassembled (don't ask me how I know).

 

cpu_micro_pool_stacked_220.gif: PNG image, 897 x 393, 8-bit/color RGBA, non-interlaced
cpu_micro_pool_stacked_227.gif: PNG image, 897 x 393, 8-bit/color RGBA, non-interlaced
cpu_micro_pool_stacked_307.gif: PNG image, 897 x 407, 8-bit/color RGBA, non-interlaced

 

Personal effects include the propellor head, the ponytail, the tie-dye shirt, and the 30-second stopwatch (thanks greatly to Ray Harryhausen for the novel model approach).

Selfie Cell

Notes & Comments:

  • NetBSD pkgsrc: Package gifsicle-1.93 has a denial-of-service vulnerability, see https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2023-44821
  • https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kohler/gifsicle/
  • Full tenure for the win
  • The "par" man page: "If I knew of any bugs, I wouldn't release the package. Of course,
    there may be bugs that I haven't yet discovered."
  • gifsicle (icicle, popsicle, dreamsicle, hammer and sickle ...)
  • One entry per person, this is it.

entry.title: Move Service from Free Plan to Standard Plan (BTP)
entry.content:
Hi experts,

we developed a service in a free plan and want to move this service now to our standard plan. I haven't found any information about how to do this. We don't want to change the free plan into a standard plan!

Is there any information of how to do this?

Thanks, Sebastian

entry.title: Re: New video series for SAP Datasphere data marketplace
entry.content:
Hi Hemanta,

 

thanks for the feedback, I will definitely include a video showing the exact setup. 
As far as I understood from your question and the link you included, you are referring to the creation of an content aggregator, which is an additional concept to group and manage data providers.

Please note, that this is not a prerequisite for setting up data exchange between two tenants. To achieve the exchange you have to follow this high-level process:

1. create a Data Provider in the source tenant, with the marketplace visibility set to private data provider and/or internal data provider (depending on your use case)

2. create an internal / private context (see video part 4)

3. create the needed data products and set the context within it (see video 5)

4. go the the context management and create activation keys according to you use case (member activation keys to enable data consumption, provider activation keys to enable data sharing) 

5. in the target tenant: activate the context --> for membership go to the "join" button within "data marketplace/my contexts/", for data sharing go to "join"-button within "data sharing cockpit/context

Hope that helped, let me know in case there are some questions left open. 
As said, I will try to publish a video explaining this in detail as soon as possible.

Thanks and regards

Florian
management

entry.title: Re: Setting up SAP PKI in BTP KYMA
entry.content:
I'm not sure. PKI services are handled internally by a dedicated team. Customers don't have access to it. 

Are you referring to key services from SAP Data Custodian?

entry.title: Re: How SAP SD module is implemented at Maritime Ports?
entry.content:
I am not sure if a simple answer can be provided to your question on a Public forum. 

How SAP is implemented? It will follow the selected Project methodology steps. 

In terms of which SAP Modules will be implemented, this depends on what part of the Business is to be covered on SAP, what SAP Suite offers and discussions between Solution Architects involved.

entry.title: Re: SXI_MONITOR: filter tested in SXMS_LMS_CONF, but not found by SXMS_EXTRACT_MESSAGES
entry.content:
I went through SXMS_EXTRACT_MESSAGES and found that type B filters only applied. That mean that I should set both check-boxes "Extract during message processing" and "Extract using external job" on Search Criteria for Selected Filter in SXMS_LMS_CONF.

But despite the fact that the filter is successfully tested and the index is updated, I could not get any message listed in output applying the filter in SXI_MONITOR.

entry.title: Re: In-House Repair in S/4HANA Service - Start with an Overview
entry.content:
Hi @Udo 

We are implmenting In-house repair scenario.

We need to know if exist, before the confirmation completed, a scenario in which a storage dispatches spare parts that are planned in service order?

For instance, a service order is planned in wich contains a service with spare parts, but the techician go to a storage in wich a person dispatch this spare part so as to later confirm with the service confirmation.

If it doesn´t exist this scenario, wich is the best way to do that?

We keep in touch wit "Advance Shipment functionality in Service Order" but this works for service order not for repair orders and works for consignment stock for customer.

We need to know if exist something like Advance shipment in service order for in-house repair.

Thanks so much for your collaboration.

Fernando

entry.title: In Cond Contract settlement process need to consider Contracts with PGI date GT settlement date
entry.content:
Experts,

Have a requirment in Settlement Management.

Current process:In condition contract settlement process via tcode WB2R_SV, currently system considers only the condition contracts whose PGI date is within the settlement date.

Expected :The system should als allow to settle the condition contracts whose PGI date is greater than settlement date.

Tried few BADIs with WB2_CC* but no luck. would be helpful if someone faced/implemented similar requirment.

entry.title: Re: LICKING THE GIF CYCLE
entry.content:
Link to the index page for gifsicle on NetBSD package source, for reference:

(old, out of service)

ftp://ftp.netbsd.org/pub/NetBSD/packages/pkgsrc/graphics/gifsicle/README.html

(new, working 2024)

https://ftp.netbsd.org/pub/pkgsrc/current/pkgsrc/graphics/gifsicle/index.html

entry.title: QM- Results recording
entry.content:
Dear Experts, 

I have a requirement from our internal customer and gain better understanding on how the data entry requirements are driven my the MIC Creation in the QM Module. Our production team has asked engineering to aid them in reducing the burden of entering MIC data during inspection of a device we manufacture inhouse. For this model line we have a shop order QTY of 1920 EA with a total of 12 MICs required over 23,000 check boxes of Pass or Fail to be entered per shop order, which run multiple times per week. The team is looking into what flexibility there may be in the QM system architecture that may allow them to simply ender ‘QTY Accepted’ and ‘QTY Rejected’ specifically for attributive (P/F) Functional Inspections, rather than using the auto generated 1,920 Pass or Fail boxes that populate when entering the MIC #. 

Any ideas are welcome. Simply put, I like to know is there any simpler way to minimize results recording for 1920 characters with ex. 1000 QTY Pass , 920 QTY Fails.

entry.title: Re: How do we confirm yield and scrap manually in workcen...
entry.content: Thank you so much! This really helps

entry.title: Re: Mass deletion of previous demand/requirements
entry.content: Thanks to Anup for this very helpful note!

entry.title: Blog Series- 1: SAP C4C Service cloud V2 integration with S4 Hana Utility (ISU)
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I am starting a new blog series for service v2 integration with S4 Hana Utility and in the very first part I am going to explain some of the key features of Service V2, pros and cons - V1 and V2 and Service v2 utility key functionality, Utility Iflows and synchronous webservice name

I am sure everyone is aware of the Buzz word “V2” and some of the Key features of V2, Listing down few of them here-

Key Features of V2 Service Cloud

  • Uninterrupted solution availability by leveraging modern technology and Hyperscaler experience.
  • API-first/microservices based services
  • Complement the core solution with tool sets to quickly deliver new composable apps
  • Almost Zero Downtime
  • Agent Desktop
  • Case management
  • Robust Global search
  • Native Email Management

[Will Publish another blog series for v2 features in detail]

Pros and Cons

V2 vs V1

Pro’s

Con’s

SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2

•  Microservices architecture – enables improved performance, scalability and flexibility.

•       Advanced business capabilities, including native intelligent sales and service solutions.

•       Latest version for Sales and Service – benefit from future innovations from SAP

•       Industry solution for utilities lacks some features available in V1. However, per SAP, it is expected to be at feature parity with V1 by the end of 2024.

SAP Sales and Service Cloud V1

•       Industry solution for utilities is mature relative to V2, and several large utility companies are already live on it.

•       Performance may be an issue while accessing information related to large customers/ accounts.

Disclaimer- This is entirely based on current V2 and V1 solution and solely my inputs. Both V2 and V1 is equally robust product.

Prerequisites for integration of Service cloud V2 with S4 Hana System

  • Software component IS-UT 808
  • OSS Note 3450264 needs to implement to get all new features of SAP Service Cloud Version 2 release.

SAP Service Cloud Version 2, Utilities Functionality 

Please refer the image below 

Service IFLOW Details

  Service Cloud V2 Integration with SAP S4 HANA Utilities consist of three different packages.

  1. SAP Service Cloud V2 for Utilities Master Data Integration with SAP S/4HANA
  2. SAP Service Cloud V2 for Utilities Premise Integration with SAP S/4HANA
  3. SAP Service Cloud V2 for Utilities Contract Account Integration with SAP S/4HANA

Master data Integration is same as V1 and there is no change in the Webservice while there is change in the contract account replication.

Webservice Name

Business partner- Same as V1

Contract account- CO_FKK_MDG_CONTRACT_ACCOUNT_BU

Premise - CO_UTILITIES_TECHNICAL_OBJEST

 Webservices for synchronous services are different from V1, and we need to use the V2 version for integrating with Service cloud v2. Please check SAP NOTE- 3253818 to see the complete list provided by SAP, which is having all the service details with the Integration flow Name, SAP Cloud Integration Source Path, Source System, Target System URL, S/4HANA Service Name 

Topic for Part 2- Some of the key difference with respect to Integration of V1 and V2 with S4 Hana system, Replication model, Details of the webservices Configuration and  etc*. Stay tuned to get further deep dive. Please provide your feedback and comments.

Thank you 

Chanchal Jha

I am an SAP ABAP Developer with 9+ years of experience. I had to take multiple breaks in my career due to relocation along with my family across the globe. I am looking forward to bounce back in my career. As a woman, I believe I can get great opportunity if I can upskill myself. 

Thank you so much for this opportunity to apply for free certification attempt.

Regards,

Neethu Lohi

entry.title: Re: Issues faced in the Building Side-by-Side Extensions...
entry.content:
Hello  @Elroy , @Ivan-Mirisola,

could you please help me to find a solution within the same context: I am implementing Side-by-Side Extension on SAP BTP using CAP.

When trying to connect to the API for BusinessPartners entity I receive this error:

[cds] - Error: Request to remote service failed.
    at run (/home/user/projects/risk-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/utils/client.js:234:31)
    at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)
    at async RemoteService.on_handler (/home/user/projects/risk-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/Service.js:265:20)
    at async next (/home/user/projects/risk-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:68:17)
    at async RemoteService.handle (/home/user/projects/risk-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:66:10)
    at async RemoteService.handle (/home/user/projects/risk-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/Service.js:292:22)
    at async ApplicationService.<anonymous> (/home/user/projects/risk-management/srv/risk-service.js:59:12)
    at async next (/home/user/projects/risk-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:68:17)
    at async ApplicationService.handle (/home/user/projects/risk-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:66:10)
    at async ApplicationService.handle (/home/user/projects/risk-management/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/common/Service.js:87:16) {
  statusCode: 502,
  reason: AggregateError [ECONNREFUSED]: 
      at internalConnectMultiple (node:net:1116:18)
      at afterConnectMultiple (node:net:1683:7)
      at TCPConnectWrap.callbackTrampoline (node:internal/async_hooks:130:17) {
    code: 'ECONNREFUSED',
    errors: [ [Error], [Error] ],
    message: 'Request to remote service failed.',
    config: {
      timeout: 10000,
      xsrfCookieName: 'XSRF-TOKEN',
      xsrfHeaderName: 'X-XSRF-TOKEN',
      maxContentLength: -1,
      maxBodyLength: -1,
      proxy: false,
      method: 'get',
      baseURL: 'http://localhost:39545/odata/v4/api-business-partner',
      url: "/A_BusinessPartner?$select=BusinessPartner,BusinessPartnerFullName&$orderby=BusinessPartner%20asc&$filter=LastName%20ne%20''%20and%20FirstName%20ne%20''&$top=1000",
      data: undefined
    },
    request: {
      method: 'GET',
      url: "http://localhost:39545/odata/v4/api-business-partner/A_BusinessPartner?$select=BusinessPartner,BusinessPartnerFullName&$orderby=BusinessPartner%20asc&$filter=LastName%20ne%20''%20and%20FirstName%20ne%20''&$top=1000",
      headers: [Object]
    },
    correlationId: '0777953d-190a-4db8-b39f-3def96db2075',
    toJSON: [Function (anonymous)]
  },
  id: '1899257',
  level: 'ERROR',
  timestamp: 1718637330838
}


The strange thing is that the cds is trying to get the API using this wrong url:

[cds] - connect to API_BUSINESS_PARTNER > odata { url: 'http://localhost:39545/odata/v4/api-business-partner' }

 

When the destination in package.json is set to the correct one:

 "cds": {
    "requires": {
      "API_BUSINESS_PARTNER": {
        "kind": "odata-v2",
        "model": "srv/external/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER",
        "credentials": {
          "url": "https://sandbox.api.sap.com/s4hanacloud/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/"
        }
      }
    }
  },

Thank you in advance,

Best regards!

entry.title: Re: SAP Data and Analytics Forum 12.-13.11.2024
entry.content:
Hi @Johann 

Are the sessions in Walldorf offered only in German or (also) in English?

Regards,

Sven

entry.title: Explore cost-efficiently the latest SAP S/4HANA Build with Guided Beta SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02
entry.content:
What is Guided Beta Testing?

Guided Beta is a program that provides SAP customers with the opportunity to test the latest SAP S/4HANA build before its official release. During this testing phase, customers can explore the new features and capabilities of the latest version of S/4HANA in their own system landscape, using their own data.

The benefits of Guided Beta Testing are numerous.
Firstly, it enables businesses to explore new products and releases cost-efficiently before the official release.
Additionally, the program provides customers with the opportunity to test their individual processes with their data in their system landscape, reducing risk and saving costs and time.

Guided Beta Testing also allows organizations to expand their individual SAP knowledge and network. By participating in the program, businesses can gain a deeper understanding of the latest features and capabilities and connect with other SAP users to exchange ideas and best practices.

Guided Beta is:

  • A Proof of Concept with the latest non-released S/4HANA Build before Release-to-Customer.
  • Specific new feature(s) introduced with the latest release of S/4HANA will be tested. End to end Regression test scenarios cannot be supported due to the limited timeframe of the program.
  • With Direct Solution Management & Development Involvement and free of charge support from SAP.

Within the Beta phase, customers will get access to the prototype of the software components listed below, as well as the complementary documentation:

  • SAP S/4HANA PRIVATE CLOUD 2023 FPS02 (Only relevant for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Customers)
  • SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 (Only relevant for SAP S/4HANA On-Premise Customers)

Other common software components for both categories of customers include:

  • SAP S/4HANA FOUNDATION 2023 FPS02
  • ABAP PLATFORM 2023 SPS02
  • SAP FIORI FOR SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPSS02
  • SAP FIORI FES 2023 for S/4HANA SPS02
  • SL TOOLSET 1.0 SPS41
          - SUM 2.0 SP20
          - SWPM 2.0 SP18

What is required from the customer?

To participate in the Guided Beta program, customers must commit to the rules and regulations of the program by signing a Test and Evaluation Agreement (TEA). They must also involve their resources in testing and provide a test system landscape, including remote access. Customers must test their processes and provide valid feedback, as the program aims to improve the platform before its official release.

Guided Beta Testing is an excellent opportunity to stay ahead of the competition by exploring the latest SAP S/4HANA build before its official release. This program provides customers with the ability to test their individual processes and provide feedback, which can help SAP to improve and provide better services to its customers.

More details can be found in this presentation.

If you’re interested in joining the program, fill out the questionnaire and  apply before July 26th, 2024 at: Beta_S4HANA@sap.com

entry.title: How to enable PDF Preview in smart form print preview
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I can observe PDF Preview is disabled in my smart form output. Can some one help me to how i can i enable this option ? can we fix it via abap ?

entry.title: SAP B1 10 why items weight and size are mandatory?
entry.content:
On SAP B1 9.x we could create an item and not fill the weight and size.
With SAP B1 10, it looks like it is mandatory.

It is not convenient, so am I missing a configuration specification that would allow us to create an item with empty size and weight? or is it really mandatory?

entry.title: Mapping License with roles
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We currently try to optimize the assigned named user license types for standard and custom roles and other authorization objects.

My current understanding already gives me the possibility to show me TCodes and the ACTVT corresponding to a certain role (AGR_1251 table), however when I now try to assign the most efficient license type (one that for example covers only the transactions granted to the role) either on the role or the used transactions, how could I get the information which license type covers which TCode / role from the SAP system?

As in the role-based user classification, I am able to see if a license covers a certain user with certain role, I would like to know if there is a way to understand which transactions (TCodes) or roles are covered by which license type.

In the rule-based user classification, one can define rules that provide you with a license type suitable for your rule set, but how is this matching established?

I would be grateful for any hints, no matter how far-fetched they are, whether it is a link between license types and standard roles, license types and transactions or even a link I am not aware of.

Thanks in advance and kind regards,

Johnny

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello,

I identify as a person in the below categories:

    • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 
    • People looking to switch their careers mid-life

I have a background in business administration and have been working in this field for 5 years. Recently, I realized how important ERP systems are, and I’m really interested in becoming an ERP consultant. I have some experience using SAP in my current job, and it has sparked my interest in learning more about it.Switching careers mid-life can be challenging, but I am very motivated. My goal is to move from being an accountant to an ERP consultant, and I believe that gaining SAP certification will help me achieve this. This opportunity to learn and get certified in SAP is something I don’t want to miss this opportunity.

Thank you for providing us this opportunity.

Best regard,

Anh Duc Le

entry.title: Re: Table and Field name for Invoice date
entry.content: I have a doubt about FKDAT being the billing date. Is the billing date the same as the invoice date in this case? Additionally, is the last invoice date determined by the maximum of the billing date? Can anyone clarify this for me?

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud, token expired
entry.content:
Hi All,

i'm working Business Application Studio for HANA Cloud, When i try to add a table in the Node, it is giving the below error.

Failed to update request header for header key authorization: failed to get cloud foundry token: could not retrieve the Cloud Foundry token: CF/XS CLI error (3001): cf command failed, reason=The token expired, was revoked, or the token ID is incorrect. Please log back in to re-authenticate

Could someone please help me.

entry.title: Creating a personal development environment #1 - Why Neovim?
entry.content:
In this blog post series I want to share my journey on how I created my personal development environment for developing cloud native SAP applications. The focus here lies in the term "journey", as I am nowhere near a finished state and still learning a lot along the way. This is not a tutorial about Neovim (others can do that better) - this is more of a documentation of my journey with a bit of focus on SAP specific things.

As most developers these days, I was using VS Code for a long time and was not really thinking about my development environment much. VS Code is a fantastic IDE (integrated development environment) and most of the features one could ask for available out of the box, such as syntax highlighting, language servers, an integrated terminal, and a wide range of available extensions. It was not until some of my programming heroes kept talking about neovim over and over again and how great it was, until I became curious. Neovim is a Vim-based text editor, and it was just amazing to see how fast and efficient these people where with it, mostly because of not using the mouse at all and the great customization options. TJ DeVries established the term "personal development environment", and I realized I hadn't personalized my previous IDE (VS Code) at all and had almost no clue about the available keyboard shortcuts. After all, the mouse was always right there so I was not forced to learn any keyboard shortcuts. So I realized to become fast and efficient I had to go down the Neovim rabbit hole - and here we are.

At first, I was very underwhelmed with the experience. After all, Neovim keybindings are very unique and unusual (from a modern perspective) and almost every feature has to be configured explicitly. A raw Neovim installation therefore is exactly that - very raw. But the plugin ecosystem for Neovim is fantastic and there are plenty of resources available to learn how to configure Neovim (see the end of this post). So I started doing my homework, hacking away, and configuring my dotfiles.

As you can see in the screen shot above, Neovim comes with a file explorer out of the box, but it doesn't look like a file explorer you know from other environments where it would show on the left of the screen and always be visible. I knew I wanted to recreate what I was used to and needed a plugin for that. The plugin ecosystem of Neovim is great and it is fairly easy to install them - not VS Code easy, but fairly easy.

You need a packager manager to install plugins/packages (I'll use these terms interchangeably) and a popular choice is lazy.nvim. In my central Neovim configuration directory (~/.config/neovim/), I created an init.lua file with following code to install lazy.nvim:

local lazypath = vim.fn.stdpath("data") .. "/lazy/lazy.nvim"

if not vim.loop.fs_stat(lazypath) then
  vim.fn.system({
    "git",
    "clone",
    "--filter=blob:none",
    "https://github.com/folke/lazy.nvim.git",
    "--branch=stable", -- latest stable release
    lazypath,
  })
end

vim.opt.rtp:prepend(lazypath)

-- this looks for all lua files in the lua/plugins/ directory
require("lazy").setup("plugins")

Ok, lazy.nvim is now installed and as you can see in the code, it apparently looks for a plugins/ directory to find all plugins. But let's take a step back and appreciate the fact that we are to write this code in Lua, which is a powerful, efficient, lightweight, embeddable scripting language. Its syntax is similar to JavaScript and it's very easy to use.

I was now ready to install my first plugin. I created a new file at ~/.config/nvim/lua/plugins/filetree.lua (the file name itself doesn't really matter) with the following content:

vim.g.loaded_netrw = 1
vim.g.loaded_netrwPlugin = 1

return {
    {
        "nvim-tree/nvim-tree.lua",
        config = function()
            require("nvim-tree").setup({
                -- call empty function to not apply default and interfere with telescope
                on_attach = function() end,
                git = {
                    ignore = false
                }
            })

            local api = require "nvim-tree.api"

            -- reimplement some of the default keybindings only for NvimTree files
            vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd("FileType", {
                pattern = { "NvimTree" },
                callback = function()
                    vim.keymap.set("n", "<Enter>", api.node.open.edit, { buffer = true })
                    vim.keymap.set("n", "fv", api.node.open.vertical, { buffer = true })
                    vim.keymap.set("n", "r", api.fs.rename, { buffer = true })
                    vim.keymap.set("n", "d", api.fs.remove, { buffer = true })
                    vim.keymap.set("n", "n", api.fs.create, { buffer = true })
                end
            })

            vim.keymap.set("n", "fe", ":NvimTreeFocus<Enter>")
        end
    },
    {
        "nvim-tree/nvim-web-devicons"
    }
}

This code disables the default netrw file explorer (lines 1 and 2) and then returns a lua table that lazy.nvim is on the lookout for. The nvim-tree plugin as well as the corresponding icons (nvim-web-devicons) get installed and while being at it, I also set up a few keybindings I want to use with nvim-tree. This is where the personalization and the quest for a "personal development environment" really starts. I decided to map rename, remove, and create to the first letters of the terms I associate them with, so r -> rename, d -> delete (remove), and n -> new (create).
I also decided to map more global operations that have to do with the file explorer to keybindings that start with the letter f (for file in my head). I use this same approach for other things like the language server and comments too, but we will get to that. This principle has really helped me learn and memorize the new keybindings fast. So to navigate to the file explorer is use fe -> file explorer and to open a file in a new vertical window I use fv -> file vertical.

This is what my Neovim looked like with nvim-tree file explorer opened. Still pretty rough, but it's a good starting point to continue personalizing it. We will continue with some miscellaneous configuration (for example a nice color scheme) in the next post.

Resources

  • typecraft on YouTube
  • DevOnDuty on YouTube

entry.title: Re: CloudEvents at SAP 🌁
entry.content: @Lidia-Sarasua, a public EDA strategy document is in the works, and that should help you highlight the differences and help decide which one to select.

entry.title: Re: CloudEvents at SAP 🌁
entry.content:
@BrianChanthat's a very good question.... deciding when to use data or notification events. As you correctly highlighted, there are performance and security considerations, and it might just end up depending on your needs. Security is a critical aspect, and in some platforms, it might be possible to establish ACLs, but then we add another layer of complexity there. I guess there is no right or wrong answer; sometimes a notification event might be enough. In other scenarios, the payload data might not be sensitive, and a data event will be great. Then there are times when you don't know when the event will be consumed and if it is not consumed in real-time the data might become stale/irrelevant if processed later.

There are many factors to take into consideration, and there might not be a straightforward answer to this dilemma.

entry.title: Re: Statement of Direction - SAP Data Services and SAP Information Steward, as of November 2023
entry.content: All of us - Data Services Consultants are worried about our careers due to vague and unclear information on future of Data services. Since 3 months, we have no relevant SAP certification to pursue and have no idea when will SAP think about us.. How could our certification was removed without a replacement in place? This is extremely disappointing from SAP. We can't loose our credibility, and SAP must think about thousands of Data Services consultants. Is this move done because SAP has decided to promote Syniti now?

entry.title: Output Parameter Determination for Production Order Output
entry.content:
Hi,

Upon accessing the manufacturing (production) order output thru Output Parameter Determination (OPD), it has Manufacturting_OP_Activity as one of the output type. However, when I tried to add getting an error message 'Selected output type MANUFACTURING_OP_ACTIVITY is not valid for this application'. 

Other than manufacturing order application, I could not find where Manufacturting_OP_Activity can be used.

Please provide how it can be utilized as an output for S/4 Public Cloud as customer has a requirement to print WIP label for a production order.

Thanks,

Falgun Shah

entry.title: Re: How to enable PDF Preview in smart form print preview
entry.content: My 2 cents: possibly, it's not available from the spool display, it's available only when the Smart Form is generated with print preview requested.

entry.title: Re: Spool file authority setting
entry.content:
Hello,

printing the device list with SPAD worked with the enhancement in GET_PRINT_PARAMETERS. I found the list with a filled authorization field in the spool output. 

Kind regards

Jan

entry.title: Re: Modify Z* Db table not working
entry.content:
Hi Raymond, I am using 'Commit work' if there is at least one update. Even if i don't use commit, its updating 1000's of recs.

What is LOB here?
